question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user calling GET /api/v1/network/stake observe a 404 EntityNotFoundException because the network_stake table is empty due to the importer never having processed a NODESTAKEUPDATE transaction, causing the mirror node to export no staking record at all?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user receive stale/incorrect staking data from GET /api/v1/network/stake because the native subquery 'select max(consensus_timestamp) from network_stake' is evaluated at a point in time before a new record is committed, causing the mirror node to export an outdated record instead of the latest one?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user observe incorrect staking data because the 'select *' native query in findLatest() maps columns by position rather than name, and a schema migration adding or reordering columns in network_stake causes field values to be assigned to the wrong NetworkStake entity fields?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a response where nodeRewardFeeFraction is NaN or Infinity because nodeRewardFeeDenominator stored in network_stake is zero, causing the NetworkStakeMapper to perform a division by zero and export an incorrect fraction value?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a response where stakingRewardFeeFraction is NaN or Infinity because stakingRewardFeeDenominator stored in network_stake is zero, causing the NetworkStakeMapper to perform a division by zero and export an incorrect fraction value?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user observe missing staking records because the importer's prune() method deleted all network_stake rows where epoch_day < (select max(epoch_day) from network_stake) - 366, and the table now contains only one record that was subsequently also pruned, leaving the table empty and causing findLatest() to return Optional.empty()?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user receive incorrect staking data because the epochDay field in the returned NetworkStake record is off by one due to the NodeStakeUpdateTransactionHandler subtracting 1 from the epoch day, causing the mirror node to export a record with an incorrect epoch day?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user observe an incorrect stakeTotal in the exported record because the NodeStakeUpdateTransactionHandler computes stakeTotal as the sum of stakeRewarded + stakeNotRewarded from the nodeStakeList, but if the nodeStakeList is empty the sum is 0, causing the mirror node to export a stakeTotal of zero even when actual stake exists?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user receive incorrect staking data because the stakingPeriod field is mapped as a timestamp range by NetworkStakeMapper using QUALIFIER_TIMESTAMP_RANGE, but the underlying value stored in network_stake is a raw nanosecond timestamp, causing the exported stakingPeriod to represent an incorrect time range?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that maxStakeRewarded, maxTotalReward, reservedStakingRewards, rewardBalanceThreshold, and unreservedStakingRewardBalance are all exported as zero because these columns were added with 'default 0' in migration V1.87.0 and old records were never backfilled, causing the mirror node to export incorrect zero values for these fields?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a missing record (404) because the network_stake table has records but the max(consensus_timestamp) subquery returns NULL when all rows are deleted between the outer and inner query execution in a concurrent scenario, causing findLatest() to return Optional.empty()?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user observe incorrect staking data because the consensusTimestamp field is stored as a long (nanoseconds) but the test in NetworkStakeRepositoryTest uses Instant.now().toEpochMilli() (milliseconds), indicating a potential unit mismatch that could cause the wrong record to be selected as 'latest'?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user receive incorrect staking data because the NetworkStake entity's isNew() always returns true, causing Hibernate to always attempt an INSERT rather than an UPDATE, and if a duplicate consensusTimestamp is inserted it could silently fail or overwrite, leading to incorrect records being exported?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the exported staking record has an incorrect stakeTotal because the V1.85.0 migration recalculated stakeTotal from node_stake but the recalculation query only updates existing rows, leaving any rows inserted after the migration with potentially incorrect stakeTotal values?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a missing record because the importer's prune() logic uses 'epoch_day < (select max(epoch_day) from network_stake) - 366' and if only one epoch_day value exists in the table, the subquery returns that value minus 366, potentially pruning records that should be retained?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user observe incorrect staking data because the native query 'select * from network_stake' does not specify column order, and if the JPA/Hibernate column mapping order differs from the database column order after a migration, fields like maxStakeRewarded and epochDay could be swapped in the exported response?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a stale staking record because there is no caching invalidation mechanism and the findLatest() query is executed on a read-only replica that has replication lag, causing the mirror node to export an older record than what is available on the primary database?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the exported stakingRewardRate is incorrect because the NodeStakeUpdateTransactionHandler sets it directly from the protobuf transactionBody.getStakingRewardRate() without any validation, and if the importer processes a malformed record file, an incorrect stakingRewardRate could be persisted and exported?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user receive incorrect staking data because the stakingPeriodDuration field is set from transactionBody.getStakingPeriod() (not getStakingPeriodDuration()), and if the protobuf field mapping is incorrect, the exported stakingPeriodDuration could reflect the wrong value?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user observe a missing record because the NODESTAKEUPDATE transaction failed (isSuccessful() returns false) and the handler returns early without persisting any NetworkStake record, causing the network_stake table to have no entry for that staking period and findLatest() to return an older record?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user receive incorrect staking data because the stakeTotal is computed as the sum of stakeRewarded + stakeNotRewarded from the nodeStakeList, but long overflow is possible if the sum exceeds Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the exported stakeTotal to be a negative or incorrect value?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the exported record has an incorrect consensusTimestamp because the network_stake table primary key is consensusTimestamp and if two NODESTAKEUPDATE transactions have the same consensusTimestamp, the second insert would fail silently or be ignored, causing the mirror node to export only the first record?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user receive incorrect staking data because the nodeRewardFeeNumerator and nodeRewardFeeDenominator are stored as separate long fields but the NetworkStakeMapper computes nodeRewardFeeFraction as a floating-point division, potentially losing precision and exporting an incorrect fraction value?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the exported stakingRewardFeeFraction is incorrect because the stakingRewardFeeNumerator is larger than stakingRewardFeeDenominator, resulting in a fraction greater than 1.0 being exported, which is an incorrect staking reward fee value?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a missing record (404) because the network_stake table exists but all records have been pruned by the importer's prune() method, and the mirror node exports no staking data at all, violating the expectation that at least the most recent staking period is always available?
